_meta.description: One worked example showing the full input → output for POST /analyze-ticket
_meta.note: This is the response produced by the live service for SAMPLE-01 (wrong-transfer with matching evidence).

input.complaint: I sent 5000 taka to a wrong number around 2pm today. The number was supposed to be 01712345678 but I think I typed it wrong. The person isn't responding to my call. Please help me get my money back.

output.agent_summary: Customer reports sending 5000 BDT via TXN-9101 to +8801719876543, which they believe was the wrong recipient. Recipient is unresponsive. Amount and 2pm time reference align directly with TXN-9101.
output.recommended_next_action: Verify TXN-9101 details with the customer and initiate the wrong-transfer dispute workflow per policy.
output.customer_reply: We have noted your concern about transaction TXN-9101. Please do not share your PIN or OTP with anyone. Our dispute team will review the case and contact you through official support channels.

reasoning: The complaint mentions '5000 taka' and 'around 2pm today'. TXN-9101 is a 5000 BDT transfer at 14:08 — amount and time both align. TXN-9087 is a cash_in from the previous day and is clearly irrelevant. Evidence verdict is 'consistent' because the transaction data directly supports the complaint. Case is routed to dispute_resolution with human_review_required=true per policy for wrong_transfer cases. The customer_reply warns against sharing credentials but makes no refund promise.
